location.description: MariaDB connection URI: mysql+mariadb://[user[:password]@]host[:port][/database]. If a database path is included, it is used as the restore target for single-database dumps.

host.description: MariaDB server hostname (default: 127.0.0.1). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: MariaDB server port (default: 3306). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: MariaDB username. Overrides the username in the location URI.

password.description: MariaDB password. Overrides the password in the location URI.

database.description: Target database for restore. If omitted, the database name is inferred from the dump filename (e.g. mydb.sql → mydb). Not used when restoring an all.sql dump.

create_db.description: When true, issues CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS before restoring a single-database dump. Defaults to false.

force.description: When true, passes --force to mariadb so restore continues after SQL errors. Defaults to false.

mariadb_bin_dir.description: Directory containing the MariaDB client binary (mariadb). When omitted, the binary is resolved via $PATH.

ssl_mode.description: SSL/TLS mode for the connection (--ssl-mode). Default: not set (server decides).

ssl_cert.description: Path to the client SSL certificate file (PEM format), passed via --ssl-cert.

ssl_key.description: Path to the client SSL private key file (PEM format), passed via --ssl-key.

ssl_ca.description: Path to the CA certificate used to verify the server (PEM format), passed via --ssl-ca.
